title: BPE
content:
Byte Pair Encoding
1. Usage:
Tokenizer.
Train on a large corpus first, then inference during usage.
2. Working Principle:
Break all words to character level, each word’s end + </w>.
Pair characters according to the frequency.
The final generation is the resulted vocabulary and group rules.
Hyperparameter K: how many grouping operations.
3. Example:
Corpus: low (×5), lower (×2), newest (×6), widest (×3)
Initial representations:
low → l o w </w>, lower → l o w e r </w>
newest → n e w e s t </w>, widest → w i d e s t </w>
Merge steps:
(a) (e,s) → es count: 9
(b) (es,t) → est count: 9
(c) (est,</w>) → est</w> count: 9
(d) (l,o) → lo count: 7
(e) (lo,w) → low count: 7
(f) (e,w) → ew count: 6
(g) (ew,est</w>) → ewest</w> count: 6
(h) (n,ewest</w>) → newest</w> count: 6
FinalvocabularyV = {l, o, w, e, r, n, s, t, i, d, </w>, es, est, est</w>, lo, low, ew, ewest</w>, newest</w>}
8
1

Inference on unseen word lowest:
Apply merge rules in order ⇒
lowest → [low, est</w>]
4. K Effect:
K = 0: only characters.
K = ∞: full word level — e.g. lower, lowest are 2 separate words.
2
